NKJV.1: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and such sexual immorality as is not even named among the Gentiles—that a man has his father’s wife!
NKJV.2: And you are puffed up, and have not rather mourned, that he who has done this deed might be taken away from among you.
NKJV.3: For I indeed, as absent in body but present in spirit, have already judged (as though I were present) him who has so done this deed.
NKJV.4: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, when you are gathered together, along with my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,
NKJV.5: deliver such a one to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.
NKJV.6: Your glorying is not good. Do you not know that a little leaven leavens the whole lump?
NKJV.7: Therefore purge out the old leaven, that you may be a new lump, since you truly are unleavened. For indeed Christ, our Passover, was sacrificed for us.
NKJV.8: Therefore let us keep the feast, not with old leaven, nor with the leaven of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
NKJV.9: I wrote to you in my epistle not to keep company with sexually immoral people.
NKJV.10: Yet I certainly did not mean with the sexually immoral people of this world, or with the covetous, or extortioners, or idolaters, since then you would need to go out of the world.
NKJV.11: But now I have written to you not to keep company with anyone named a brother, who is sexually immoral, or covetous, or an idolater, or a reviler, or a drunkard, or an extortioner— not even to eat with such a person.
NKJV.12: For what have I to do with judging those also who are outside? Do you not judge those who are inside?
NKJV.13: But those who are outside God judges. Therefore “put away from yourselves the evil person.”

ESV.1: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and of a kind that is not tolerated even among pagans, for a man has his father's wife.
ESV.2: And you are arrogant! Ought you not rather to mourn? Let him who has done this be removed from among you.
ESV.3: For though absent in body, I am present in spirit; and as if present, I have already pronounced judgment on the one who did such a thing.
ESV.4: When you are assembled in the name of the Lord Jesus and my spirit is present, with the power of our Lord Jesus,
ESV.5: you are to deliver this man to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, so that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord.
ESV.6: Your boasting is not good. Do you not know that a little leaven leavens the whole lump?
ESV.7: Cleanse out the old leaven that you may be a new lump, as you really are unleavened. For Christ, our Passover lamb, has been sacrificed.
ESV.8: Let us therefore celebrate the festival, not with the old leaven, the leaven of malice and evil, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
ESV.9: I wrote to you in my letter not to associate with sexually immoral people —
ESV.10: not at all meaning the sexually immoral of this world, or the greedy and swindlers, or idolaters, since then you would need to go out of the world.
ESV.11: But now I am writing to you not to associate with anyone who bears the name of brother if he is guilty of sexual immorality or greed, or is an idolater, reviler, drunkard, or swindler — not even to eat with such a one.
ESV.12: For what have I to do with judging outsiders? Is it not those inside the church  whom you are to judge?
ESV.13: God judges  those outside. “Purge the evil person from among you.”

NASB.1: It is actually reported that there is immorality among you, and immorality of such a kind as does not exist even among the Gentiles, that someone has his father's wife.
NASB.2: You have become arrogant and have not mourned instead, so that the one who had done this deed would be removed from your midst.
NASB.3: For I, on my part, though absent in body but present in spirit, have already judged him who has so committed this, as though I were present.
NASB.4: In the name of our Lord Jesus, when you are assembled, and I with you in spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus,
NASB.5: [I have decided] to deliver such a one to Satan for the destruction of his flesh, so that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.
NASB.6: Your boasting is not good. Do you not know that a little leaven leavens the whole lump [of dough]?
NASB.7: Clean out the old leaven so that you may be a new lump, just as you are [in fact] unleavened. For Christ our Passover also has been sacrificed.
NASB.8: Therefore let us celebrate the feast, not with old leaven, nor with the leaven of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
NASB.9: I wrote you in my letter not to associate with immoral people;
NASB.10: I [did] not at all [mean] with the immoral people of this world, or with the covetous and swindlers, or with idolaters, for then you would have to go out of the world.
NASB.11: But actually, I wrote to you not to associate with any so-called brother if he is an immoral person, or covetous, or an idolater, or a reviler, or a drunkard, or a swindler-- not even to eat with such a one.
NASB.12: For what have I to do with judging outsiders? Do you not judge those who are within [the church]?
NASB.13: But those who are outside, God judges. REMOVE THE WICKED MAN FROM AMONG YOURSELVES.

NLT.1: I can hardly believe the report about the sexual immorality going on among you — something that even pagans don’t do. I am told that a man in your church is living in sin with his stepmother.
NLT.2: You are so proud of yourselves, but you should be mourning in sorrow and shame. And you should remove this man from your fellowship.
NLT.3: Even though I am not with you in person, I am with you in the Spirit. And as though I were there, I have already passed judgment on this man
NLT.4: in the name of the Lord Jesus. You must call a meeting of the church. I will be present with you in spirit, and so will the power of our Lord Jesus.
NLT.5: Then you must throw this man out and hand him over to Satan so that his sinful nature will be destroyed and he himself will be saved on the day the Lord returns.
NLT.6: Your boasting about this is terrible. Don’t you realize that this sin is like a little yeast that spreads through the whole batch of dough?
NLT.7: Get rid of the old “yeast” by removing this wicked person from among you. Then you will be like a fresh batch of dough made without yeast, which is what you really are. Christ, our Passover Lamb, has been sacrificed for us.
NLT.8: So let us celebrate the festival, not with the old bread of wickedness and evil, but with the new bread of sincerity and truth.
NLT.9: When I wrote to you before, I told you not to associate with people who indulge in sexual sin.
NLT.10: But I wasn’t talking about unbelievers who indulge in sexual sin, or are greedy, or cheat people, or worship idols. You would have to leave this world to avoid people like that.
NLT.11: I meant that you are not to associate with anyone who claims to be a believer yet indulges in sexual sin, or is greedy, or worships idols, or is abusive, or is a drunkard, or cheats people. Don’t even eat with such people.
NLT.12: It isn’t my responsibility to judge outsiders, but it certainly is your responsibility to judge those inside the church who are sinning.
NLT.13: God will judge those on the outside; but as the Scriptures say, “You must remove the evil person from among you.”

WEB.1: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and such sexual immorality as is not even named among the Gentiles, that one has his father’s wife.
WEB.2: You are puffed up, and didn’t rather mourn, that he who had done this deed might be removed from among you.
WEB.3: For I most certainly, as being absent in body but present in spirit, have already, as though I were present, judged him who has done this thing.
WEB.4: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, you being gathered together, and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,
WEB.5: are to deliver such a one to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.
WEB.6: Your boasting is not good. Don’t you know that a little yeast leavens the whole lump?
WEB.7: Purge out the old yeast, that you may be a new lump, even as you are unleavened. For indeed Christ, our Passover, has been sacrificed in our place.
WEB.8: Therefore let us keep the feast, not with old yeast, neither with the yeast of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
WEB.9: I wrote to you in my letter to have no company with sexual sinners;
WEB.10: yet not at all meaning with the sexual sinners of this world, or with the covetous and extortionists, or with idolaters; for then you would have to leave the world.
WEB.11: But as it is, I wrote to you not to associate with anyone who is called a brother who is a sexual sinner, or covetous, or an idolater, or a slanderer, or a drunkard, or an extortionist. Don’t even eat with such a person.
WEB.12: For what do I have to do with also judging those who are outside? Don’t you judge those who are within?
WEB.13: But those who are outside, God judges. “Put away the wicked man from among yourselves.”

CSB17.1: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality <sup>ⓖ</sup> among you, and the kind of sexual immorality that is not even tolerated <sup>[1]</sup> among the Gentiles <sup>ⓗ</sup>  — a man is sleeping with his father’s wife. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.2: And you are arrogant! Shouldn’t you be filled with grief <sup>ⓙ</sup> and remove from your congregation the one who did this?
CSB17.3: Even though I am absent in the body, I am present in spirit. <sup>ⓚ</sup> As one who is present with you in this way, I have already pronounced judgment on the one who has been doing such a thing.
CSB17.4: When you are assembled in the name of our Lord Jesus, and I am with you in spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus,
CSB17.5: hand that one over to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, <sup>ⓛ</sup> so that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.6: Your boasting is not good. Don’t you know that a little leaven <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> leavens the whole batch of dough? <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.7: Clean out the old leaven so that you may be a new unleavened batch, as indeed you are. For Christ our Passover lamb <sup>ⓑ</sup> has been sacrificed. <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.8: Therefore, let us observe the feast, not with old leaven or with the leaven of malice and evil, <sup>ⓒ</sup> but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
CSB17.9: I wrote to you in a letter not to associate <sup>ⓓ</sup> with sexually immoral people. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.10: I did not mean the immoral people of this world or the greedy <sup>ⓕ</sup> and swindlers <sup>ⓖ</sup> or idolaters; otherwise you would have to leave the world. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.11: But actually, I wrote <sup>[4]</sup> you not to associate with anyone who claims to be a brother or sister <sup>ⓘ</sup> and is sexually immoral or greedy, an idolater or verbally abusive, a drunkard <sup>ⓙ</sup> or a swindler. Do not even eat with such a person.
CSB17.12: For what business is it of mine to judge <sup>ⓚ</sup> outsiders? Don’t you judge those who are inside?
CSB17.13: God judges outsiders. Remove the evil person from among you. [5] ⓛ

AMP.1: It is actually reported [everywhere] that there is sexual immorality among you, a kind of immorality that is condemned even among the [unbelieving] Gentiles: that someone has [an intimate relationship with] his father's wife.
AMP.2: And you are proud and arrogant! You should have mourned in shame so that the man who has done this [disgraceful] thing would be removed from your fellowship!
AMP.3: For I, though absent [from you] in body but present in spirit, have already passed judgment on him who has committed this [act], as if I were present.
AMP.4: In the name of our Lord Jesus, when you are assembled, and I am with you in spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus,
AMP.5: you are to hand over this man to Satan for the destruction of his body, so that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.
AMP.6: Your boasting [over the supposed spirituality of your church] is not good [indeed, it is vulgar and inappropriate]. Do you not know that [just] a little leaven ferments the whole batch [of dough, just as a little sin corrupts a person or an entire church]?
AMP.7: Clean out the old leaven so that you may be a new batch, just as you are, still unleavened. For Christ our Passover Lamb has been sacrificed.
AMP.8: Therefore, let us celebrate the feast, not with old leaven, nor with leaven of vice and malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and [untainted] truth.
AMP.9: I wrote you in my [previous] letter not to associate with [sexually] immoral people—
AMP.10: not meaning the immoral people of this world, or the greedy ones and swindlers, or idolaters, for then you would have to get out of the world and human society altogether!
AMP.11: But actually, I have written to you not to associate with any so-called [Christian] brother if he is sexually immoral or greedy, or is an idolater [devoted to anything that takes the place of God], or is a reviler [who insults or slanders or otherwise verbally abuses others], or is a drunkard or a swindler—you must not so much as eat with such a person.
AMP.12: For what business is it of mine to judge outsiders (non-believers)? Do you not judge those who are within the church [to protect the church as the situation requires]?
AMP.13: God alone sits in judgment on those who are outside [the faith]. Remove the wicked one from among you [expel him from your church].

MSG.1: I also received a report of scandalous sex within your church family, a kind that wouldn't be tolerated even outside the church: One of your men is sleeping with his stepmother.
MSG.2: And you're so above it all that it doesn't even faze you! Shouldn't this break your hearts? Shouldn't it bring you to your knees in tears? Shouldn't this person and his conduct be confronted and dealt with?
MSG.3: I'll tell you what I would do. Even though I'm not there in person, consider me right there with you, because I can fully see what's going on. I'm telling you that this is wrong. You must not simply look the other way and hope it goes away on its own. Bring it out in the open and deal with it in the authority of Jesus our Master.
MSG.4: Assemble the community—I'll be present in spirit with you and our Master Jesus will be present in power.
MSG.5: Hold this man's conduct up to public scrutiny. Let him defend it if he can! But if he can't, then out with him! It will be totally devastating to him, of course, and embarrassing to you. But better devastation and embarrassment than damnation. You want him on his feet and forgiven before the Master on the Day of Judgment.
MSG.6: Your flip and callous arrogance in these things bothers me. You pass it off as a small thing, but it's anything but that. Yeast, too, is a "small thing," but it works its way through a whole batch of bread dough pretty fast.
MSG.7: So get rid of this "yeast." Our true identity is flat and plain, not puffed up with the wrong kind of ingredient. The Messiah, our Passover Lamb, has already been sacrificed for the Passover meal, and we are the Unraised Bread part of the Feast.
MSG.8: So let's live out our part in the Feast, not as raised bread swollen with the yeast of evil, but as flat bread—simple, genuine, unpretentious.
MSG.9: I wrote you in my earlier letter that you shouldn't make yourselves at home among the sexually promiscuous.
MSG.10: I didn't mean that you should have nothing at all to do with outsiders of that sort. Or with crooks, whether blue– or white-collar. Or with spiritual phonies, for that matter. You'd have to leave the world entirely to do that!
MSG.11: But I am saying that you shouldn't act as if everything is just fine when one of your Christian companions is promiscuous or crooked, is flip with God or rude to friends, gets drunk or becomes greedy and predatory. You can't just go along with this, treating it as acceptable behavior.
MSG.12: I'm not responsible for what the outsiders do, but don't we have some responsibility for those within our community of believers?
MSG.13: God decides on the outsiders, but we need to decide when our brothers and sisters are out of line and, if necessary, clean house.

NIV.1:
Expel the Immoral Brother!
It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and of a kind that does not occur even among pagans: A man has his father’s wife.
NIV.2: And you are proud! Shouldn’t you rather have been filled with grief and have put out of your fellowship the man who did this?
NIV.3: Even though I am not physically present, I am with you in spirit. And I have already passed judgment on the one who did this, just as if I were present.
NIV.4: When you are assembled in the name of our Lord Jesus and I am with you in spirit, and the power of our Lord Jesus is present,
NIV.5: hand this man over to Satan, so that the sinful nature may be destroyed and his spirit saved on the day of the Lord.
NIV.6: Your boasting is not good. Don’t you know that a little yeast works through the whole batch of dough?
NIV.7: Get rid of the old yeast that you may be a new batch without yeast—as you really are. For Christ, our Passover lamb, has been sacrificed.
NIV.8: Therefore let us keep the Festival, not with the old yeast, the yeast of malice and wickedness, but with bread without yeast, the bread of sincerity and truth.
NIV.9: I have written you in my letter not to associate with sexually immoral people—
NIV.10: not at all meaning the people of this world who are immoral, or the greedy and swindlers, or idolaters. In that case you would have to leave this world.
NIV.11: But now I am writing you that you must not associate with anyone who calls himself a brother but is sexually immoral or greedy, an idolater or a slanderer, a drunkard or a swindler. With such a man do not even eat.
NIV.12: What business is it of mine to judge those outside the church? Are you not to judge those inside?
NIV.13: God will judge those outside. “Expel the wicked man from among you.”

NRSVCE.1: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and of a kind that is not found even among pagans; for a man is living with his father’s wife.
NRSVCE.2: And you are arrogant! Should you not rather have mourned, so that he who has done this would have been removed from among you?
NRSVCE.3: For though absent in body, I am present in spirit; and as if present I have already pronounced judgment
NRSVCE.4: in the name of the Lord Jesus on the man who has done such a thing. When you are assembled, and my spirit is present with the power of our Lord Jesus,
NRSVCE.5: you are to hand this man over to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, so that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord.
NRSVCE.6: Your boasting is not a good thing. Do you not know that a little yeast leavens the whole batch of dough?
NRSVCE.7: Clean out the old yeast so that you may be a new batch, as you really are unleavened. For our paschal lamb, Christ, has been sacrificed.
NRSVCE.8: Therefore, let us celebrate the festival, not with the old yeast, the yeast of malice and evil, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
NRSVCE.9: I wrote to you in my letter not to associate with sexually immoral persons —
NRSVCE.10: not at all meaning the immoral of this world, or the greedy and robbers, or idolaters, since you would then need to go out of the world.
NRSVCE.11: But now I am writing to you not to associate with anyone who bears the name of brother or sister who is sexually immoral or greedy, or is an idolater, reviler, drunkard, or robber. Do not even eat with such a one.
NRSVCE.12: For what have I to do with judging those outside? Is it not those who are inside that you are to judge?
NRSVCE.13: God will judge those outside. “Drive out the wicked person from among you.”

NET.1: It is actually reported that sexual immorality exists among you, the kind of immorality that is not permitted even among the Gentiles, so that someone is cohabiting with his father’s wife.
NET.2: And you are proud! Shouldn’t you have been deeply sorrowful instead and removed the one who did this from among you?
NET.3: For even though I am absent physically, I am present in spirit. And I have already judged the one who did this, just as though I were present.
NET.4: When you gather together in the name of our Lord Jesus, and I am with you in spirit, along with the power of our Lord Jesus,
NET.5: turn this man over to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, so that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord.
NET.6: Your boasting is not good. Don’t you know that a little yeast affects the whole batch of dough?
NET.7: Clean out the old yeast so that you may be a new batch of dough — you are, in fact, without yeast. For Christ, our Passover lamb, has been sacrificed.
NET.8: So then, let us celebrate the festival, not with the old yeast, the yeast of vice and evil, but with the bread without yeast, the bread of sincerity and truth.
NET.9: I wrote you in my letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
NET.10: In no way did I mean the immoral people of this world, or the greedy and swindlers and idolaters, since you would then have to go out of the world.
NET.11: But now I am writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls himself a Christian who is sexually immoral, or greedy, or an idolater, or verbally abusive, or a drunkard, or a swindler. Do not even eat with such a person.
NET.12: For what do I have to do with judging those outside? Are you not to judge those inside?
NET.13: But God will judge those outside. Remove the evil person from among you.
